  • can.liu.1996[at]gmail.com
  • Google Scholar

I am a research fellow in the College of Computing and Data Science (CCDS), Nanyang Technological University. I received my Ph.D. in Intelligent Science (2023), B.Sc. in Computer Science (2018), and B.Ec in Economics (2018) degrees from Peking University. My research focuses on AI-driven data visualization and human–computer interaction, with particular emphasis on how AI can support human understanding of complex data. Specifically, I am interested in intelligent data interaction, intelligent visual design, and intelligent data management. I also explore how AI enables more creative visual representations and supports applications in digital humanities.

I enjoy writing Chinese poetry and hiking, often at the same time. Visit Mushan Collection to read some of my ancient-style poems.

All Publications

Featured Projects

DataWink

A system that supports interactive data communication by leveraging AI to help users generate, refine, and present data-driven narratives.

Paper Demo Video
Spatial-Constraint Model

We proposed a spatial constraint model for activating static visualization.

Paper Demo Video
SmartCube

We proposed an adaptive data management architecture for the real-time visualization of spatiotemporal datasets

Paper
Proteus Mobile

Proteus is a multi-agent framework designed to automatically adapt desktop visualizations for mobile devices.

Paper Demo
PrompTHis

An interactive system that helps users explore and refine prompts for text-to-image generation through visual and iterative workflows.

Paper Demo Video
Glyph-Weaver

We proposed a visual design creativity activation model.

Demo
Historical Visualization

OldVisOnline is a collection of old visualizations published before 1950. We have been studying a variety of usage scenarios of OldVisOnline.

Paper Demo
AutoTitle

We proposed a semi-automatic method for title generation.

Paper
Show More Projects
